onboarding.question:
1. Define what "ambient awareness only" means operationally — specify whether the logged-out indicator is clickable, whether it opens any UI, and whether it contains any call-to-action or sign-up affordance.
2. Specify the exact text or messaging pattern the logged-out indicator must display to clearly communicate the unauthenticated state to the user.
3. Define the user experience when a logged-out session expires without the expiry warning surfacing — what message or state the user sees on their next interaction, and whether any recovery path exists.
4. Specify what "without visual disruption" means measurably during authenticated state resolution — define whether the UI permits loading states, spinners, re-renders of the chat thread, or full page reloads during the transition from logged-out to authenticated.
onboarding.recommendations:
[1]. My recommendation: The logged-out indicator is non-clickable, non-interactive text that does not open any UI, contain any affordance, or prompt sign-up — it is purely informational chrome.

→ Rationale: "Ambient awareness only" means the indicator communicates state without creating interaction friction; clickability or affordances would convert it from passive awareness to active UI, defeating the minimalist design principle.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[2]. My recommendation: The logged-out indicator displays the text "Not signed in" in a single line, using the muted text color (rgba(245, 245, 245, 0.6)) and 12px sans-serif font, positioned fixed in the top-right corner 16px from the top and right edges.

→ Rationale: "Not signed in" is direct and unambiguous; the specified color and sizing ensure it is perceptible without visual prominence; fixed positioning keeps it consistently visible without interfering with conversation flow.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[3]. My recommendation: When a logged-out session expires (60 minutes inactivity), the next user interaction (sending a message, tapping a button) is blocked; the UI surfaces an inline message at the top of the chat interface stating "Your session has expired. Sign in to continue" with a single "Sign in" button that opens the Auth Sheet; no conversation history is available, and no recovery path exists for the expired conversation.

→ Rationale: Clear, immediate feedback on session expiry prevents user confusion; blocking the next action rather than silently failing ensures the user understands why their action didn't complete; the sign-in CTA provides a direct path forward without further friction.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[4]. My recommendation: "Without visual disruption" means the authenticated state transition occurs with no loading spinners, skeleton screens, or full-page reloads — the logged-out UI remains fully visible and interactive while authentication state resolution occurs in the background; upon success within 2 seconds, conversation history loads silently into the existing chat interface, and the logged-out indicator disappears; the user sees no flash, flicker, or re-render.

→ Rationale: Invisible transitions maintain perceived continuity and respect the minimalist design; no loading states keep the interface clean and responsive; silent history population preserves the "conversation leads" principle.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

Once you approve these recommendations, I'll update the product spec to reflect each confirmed decision.
